content:
Let’s write 391=17·23 and set  
  L(a,b)=lcm(a,b),   L(a)=lcm(a,391).  
We want some a<391 with L(a,b)>L(a).  If gcd(a,391)=23, then  
  L(a)=lcm(a,391)=391  (since a has no 17‐factor),  
whereas for b>17 we can make L(a,b)>391.  In fact the smallest b>17 is b=18, and indeed taking a=23 gives  
  L(23,391)=391,     L(23,18)=lcm(23,18)=23·18=414>391.  
No b<18 can work (for b≤17 one checks L(a,b)≤391 always).  Hence the least possible b is  
\boxed{18}.

message.content:
Let’s write 391=17·23 and set  
  L(a,b)=lcm(a,b),   L(a)=lcm(a,391).  
We want some a<391 with L(a,b)>L(a).  If gcd(a,391)=23, then  
  L(a)=lcm(a,391)=391  (since a has no 17‐factor),  
whereas for b>17 we can make L(a,b)>391.  In fact the smallest b>17 is b=18, and indeed taking a=23 gives  
  L(23,391)=391,     L(23,18)=lcm(23,18)=23·18=414>391.  
No b<18 can work (for b≤17 one checks L(a,b)≤391 always).  Hence the least possible b is  
\boxed{18}.
